leads.name: Emily Yuan
leads.whatTheyDo: Corgi is an AI-native, full-stack insurance platform built for startups, operating as a licensed carrier and expanding into new verticals including trucking.
leads.recentNews: Corgi closed a significant funding round in May 2026, reaching a multi-billion dollar valuation and accelerating expansion into new insurance verticals.
leads.founderFocus: Emily Yuan co-founded Corgi with a focus on building AI financial infrastructure and is actively operating the company through rapid growth.
leads.teamTrajectory: The team has scaled significantly since founding, with major investor backing driving headcount and product expansion across new markets.
leads.whyFit: Emily is a co-founder and COO at a company that just completed a major funding event. A cash event of this scale creates immediate tax exposure that compliance work alone does not address. Anthony's team specializes in moving post-raise companies from compliance into proactive strategy.
leads.whyNow: A significant funding round closed in May 2026, opening a tax planning window that is most valuable in the first 90 days post-raise. The larger the cash event, the larger the exposure that standard compliance may not flag.
leads.connNote: Noticed the recent funding round at Corgi. A raise creates tax exposure most compliance CPAs do not flag proactively. I work with founders on strategy post-raise. Happy to share what that looks like.
leads.emailBody:
A funding round confirms progress.

It does not automatically confirm that every tax opportunity was explored. Compliance work confirms the forms were completed correctly. That is a different job than making sure every strategy was considered.

I work with founders post-raise to identify what proactive planning recovers. If you have 30 minutes, reply here and I will walk you through what that typically looks like at your stage.

leads.name: Anish Karyat
leads.whatTheyDo: Moment is an AI operating system for investment management, serving RIAs, broker-dealers, and private banks with tools to automate fixed income trading and wealth management workflows.
leads.recentNews: Moment closed a $78M Series C in May 2026 led by Index Ventures, bringing total funding to $134M. Clients now manage upwards of $10 trillion in assets.
leads.founderFocus: Founded by former quants and traders from Citadel Securities, the company scaled from $300B to $10T-plus in client AUM in roughly 18 months.
leads.teamTrajectory: Scaling rapidly following consecutive funding rounds, expanding its enterprise client base to include Edward Jones, LPL Financial, and Hightower Advisors.
leads.whyFit: Anish is a C-suite leader at a wealth management fintech that just completed a major funding round. Companies scaling this fast in financial services carry tax exposure that compliance-only advisors routinely miss. Anthony's team has specific experience with fintech and financial services operators.
leads.whyNow: A Series C closed in May 2026. A raise of this scale combined with growth from $300B to $10T in managed assets creates a tax planning window that is most valuable in the first 90 days.
leads.connNote: Noticed the recent raise at Moment. A cash event at this stage creates tax exposure most compliance work does not flag proactively. I work with leadership teams on post-raise strategy. Happy to connect.
leads.emailSubj: Tax strategy after a major raise
leads.emailBody:
A significant funding round changes the tax picture.

Most compliance work confirms the forms were completed correctly. It does not confirm that every strategy was explored. At the scale Moment is operating, the gap between compliance and proactive planning is often larger than it looks.

I work with leadership teams at growth-stage companies to recover what compliance leaves behind. If you have 30 minutes, reply here and I will share what that typically looks like.

leads.name: Robbie Falkenthal
leads.whatTheyDo: Wordsmith AI builds AI tools for in-house legal teams, enabling companies to self-serve legal workflows, triage contracts, and reduce dependency on outside counsel.
leads.recentNews: Wordsmith raised a $70M Series B on June 3, 2026, planning to scale to 300 employees globally and double down on the US market, serving 500-plus companies including BT, Financial Times, and Canva.
leads.founderFocus: The company is scaling aggressively into enterprise, with major client wins including Sage and Starling alongside the funding announcement.
leads.teamTrajectory: Planning to scale from current headcount to 300 globally by end of year, with US market expansion as the primary growth lever.
leads.whyFit: Robbie is the COO of a US-based company that just closed a major funding round. Post-raise cash events at companies with US operations create immediate tax planning needs. Anthony's team works with operators at this exact stage.
leads.whyNow: The Series B closed June 3, 2026, making this the most recent funding signal in the pipeline. The 90-day window post-raise is when proactive tax strategy has the most leverage.
leads.connNote: Noticed the recent Series B at Wordsmith. A raise creates immediate tax exposure most compliance work does not flag proactively. I work with operators post-raise to close that gap. Happy to connect.
leads.emailBody:
A Series B confirms momentum.

It does not automatically confirm that the tax planning caught up with the growth. Most compliance work confirms forms were filed correctly. That is not the same as every opportunity being explored.

I work with operators at post-raise companies to identify what proactive planning recovers. If you have 30 minutes, reply here and I will walk you through what that looks like at your stage.

leads.name: Scott Galla
leads.whatTheyDo: Steadily is a landlord insurance platform offering fast, affordable coverage for rental property owners across all 50 US states, with a tech-forward approach to the 18 million individual rental property owner market.
leads.recentNews: Named preferred landlord insurance provider for Real Property Management in April 2026, and recognized by CNBC as one of the best landlord insurance companies of 2026. Over $250M in annualized gross written premium.
leads.founderFocus: Company ranked No. 63 on the Inc 5000 fastest-growing private companies list in 2025 and continues aggressive national expansion.
leads.teamTrajectory: Scaling nationally with a major franchise partnership and growing finance and operations teams to support an accelerating revenue trajectory.
leads.whyFit: Scott is general counsel at a fast-growing insurance company actively building its finance team. Growth at this pace in insurance and real estate creates tax complexity that compliance-oriented advisors routinely miss. Anthony's team works with real estate and insurance adjacent clients at exactly this stage.
leads.whyNow: Steadily is actively hiring in its finance department, a signal that revenue has scaled past the threshold where compliance alone is no longer sufficient. Building a finance team without proactive tax strategy in place is a common and costly gap.
leads.connNote: Noticed Steadily is hiring in finance. That is usually the moment revenue has crossed the threshold where compliance alone stops being enough. I work with leadership teams on proactive strategy at this stage. Happy to connect.
leads.emailSubj: Building a finance team changes the tax picture
leads.emailBody:
Hiring into the finance function is a signal.

It usually means revenue has scaled to the point where compliance work alone is no longer the complete answer. Most companies at this stage assume their current advisors have it covered. Compliance and proactive strategy are not the same job.

I work with leadership teams at growing companies to close that gap. If you have 30 minutes, reply here and I will share what proactive planning typically recovers at your stage.

leads.name: Parisse Spelios
leads.whatTheyDo: Worth AI is an AI-powered onboarding and underwriting platform for financial institutions and small businesses, built on a proprietary database of 700 million-plus businesses across 200-plus countries.
leads.recentNews: Worth raised a $30M Series A in March 2026 led by Fulcrum Equity Partners, bringing total funding to $87M. Platform powers underwriting for banks, lenders, and insurance providers serving small businesses.
leads.founderFocus: Founded by Suneera Madhani and Sal Rehmetullah, serial entrepreneurs with prior exits, focused on financial inclusion and equitable underwriting at scale.
leads.teamTrajectory: Team at roughly 62 employees and scaling post-raise, with active investment in people infrastructure to support growth into enterprise financial institutions.
leads.whyFit: Parisse is a C-suite people leader at a fintech company that recently closed a Series A. Post-raise scaling creates tax exposure that compliance work does not address proactively. As the person overseeing headcount growth, she has direct visibility into the cost and complexity that Anthony's team helps manage.
leads.whyNow: Worth AI closed a $30M Series A in March 2026. At 83 days post-raise, this window is still open. Companies scaling fast post-raise often discover the tax exposure later than is ideal.
leads.connNote: Noticed Worth AI recently raised a Series A. Scaling the team post-raise creates tax exposure most compliance work does not flag. I work with operators at growth-stage companies to close that gap. Happy to connect.
leads.emailBody:
Scaling the team after a raise is the part people plan for.

The tax exposure that comes with it is usually not something compliance work flags automatically. Most operators at this stage assume their advisors have it covered. Compliance and strategy are not the same job.

I work with operators at growth-stage companies to surface what proactive planning recovers. If you have 30 minutes, reply here and I will walk you through what that typically looks like.

dashboard.pulse: Four of five leads triggered a new funding event in the last 90 days, creating a concentrated window of post-raise tax exposure across fintech, insurance, and legal AI. All five companies are US-based and scaling past the revenue threshold where compliance-only advisory leaves the most money on the table. A short guide on the top tax moves founders miss in the 90 days after a raise would land at exactly the right moment for this cohort.

signals.name: New Funding Round
signals.desc: Company secured new investment in the last 90 days, creating immediate tax exposure and a planning window that is most valuable in the first 90 days post-raise.

signals.name: Finance Team Hiring
signals.desc: Company is actively building its finance team, a signal that revenue has crossed the threshold where compliance alone is no longer the complete answer.

markets.notes: All five leads are US-based companies scaling post-raise in financial services and adjacent verticals, the exact segment where proactive tax strategy has the highest leverage relative to compliance-only advisory.
